evidence.note: Alaska CBPL states that the construction contractor program is staffed by the division and that General Contractors may perform new home construction, commercial work, and residential remodel work.
Alaska CBPL defines Residential Contractors and the Residential Contractor Endorsement for residential structures and substantial residential alteration work.
Alaska CBPL says General Contractors may perform commercial work.
Alaska CBPL states that an Electrical Administrator license is required for at least one employee of a valid Alaska contractor performing work covered by NEC or NESC codes.

limitations: Electrician certificates of fitness are regulated by the Alaska Department of Labor and remain outside this CBPL source entry.

evidence.note: Alaska CBPL lists Residential Plumbing and Hydronic Heating and Unlimited Commercial and Industrial Plumbing under Mechanical Administrator regulated professions.

limitations: Plumbers are regulated by the Alaska Department of Labor, Labor Standards and Safety; this decision tracks the CBPL administrator/contractor board path and not worker certificates.

evidence.note: Alaska CBPL lists Heating, Cooling, and Process Piping, Residential HVAC, Unlimited HVAC/Sheet Metal, and Unlimited Refrigeration categories.

Alaska CBPL states that Mechanical Contractors may perform mechanical disciplines allowed by their Mechanical Administrator license and that the administrator license is required in addition to the Mechanical Contractor license.
The official Alaska specialty contractor trades list defines Roofing Contractor as a recognized specialty contractor trade.
The official Alaska specialty contractor trades list recognizes Asbestos Abatement Contractor and notes additional Department of Labor certification requirements.

limitations: Additional Alaska Department of Labor asbestos certification evidence should be registered separately before implementing verification for asbestos-specific credentials.

evidence.note: The official Alaska specialty contractor trades list defines Sheet Metal Contractor and says HVAC ducting/equipment work is done by a mechanical or general contractor under a licensed mechanical administrator.

The official Alaska specialty contractor trades list recognizes excavation, water and sewer, and water system contractor scopes involving trenches, underground cables or piping, water lines, septic tanks, sewer lines, and water wells.
Alaska CBPL covers residential remodel work under General Contractors and Residential Contractors and defines Handyman Contractors for small projects.
Alaska's mechanical-contractor definition includes construction or installation work subject to the Uniform Swimming Pool, Spa, and Hot Tub Code.

limitations: This decision covers the statewide contractor and mechanical-administrator path; local permits and inspections remain outside the registry.

evidence.note: Alaska's mechanical-contractor definition includes construction or installation work subject to the Uniform Solar Energy Code.

limitations: Electrical work associated with solar installations may also require the separate electrical-contractor and electrical-administrator path.

evidence.note: Arizona ROC lists statewide General Engineering and General Commercial contractor classifications.
Arizona ROC states that it issues separate residential licenses and lists General Residential classifications.
Arizona ROC defines commercial contracting and lists B-1 and B-2 General Commercial classifications.
Arizona ROC lists commercial, residential, and dual electrical contractor classifications, including C-11 and CR-11.
Arizona ROC lists plumbing contractor classifications, including C-37 and C-77 Plumbing Including Solar.
Arizona ROC lists Air Conditioning and Refrigeration contractor classifications.
Arizona ROC lists mechanical scopes including boilers, steamfitting, process piping, air conditioning, and refrigeration.
Arizona ROC lists statewide commercial, residential, and dual roofing classifications.
Arizona ROC identifies solar-authorized pool, plumbing, electrical, and boiler classifications.
Arizona ROC lists General Swimming Pool, Swimming Pool Service and Repair, and spa classifications.
Arizona ADEQ describes AHERA-certified asbestos professionals and states that Arizona does not provide the required training; it does not identify a statewide asbestos contractor license register.
Arizona ROC defines C-45 and R-45 Sheet Metal contractor classifications.
Arizona ROC lists engineering, waterworks, transmission-line, excavation, sewer, drain, and pipe-laying scopes covering underground utility work.
Arizona ROC lists remodeling, repair, and Minor Home Improvements contractor classifications.
CSLB lists statewide A General Engineering and B General Building contractor classifications.
The official application lists B General Building and B-2 Residential Remodeling classifications.
The official application lists statewide A and B contractor classifications covering engineering and building work.
CSLB defines the statewide C-10 Electrical Contractor classification.
CSLB defines the statewide C-36 Plumbing Contractor classification.
The official classification guide includes C-20 Warm-Air Heating, Ventilating and Air-Conditioning.
The official guide includes C-4 boiler, C-20 HVAC, and C-38 refrigeration contractor classifications.
The official classification guide defines C-39 Roofing Contractor.
The official classification guide defines C-46 Solar Contractor and solar work within other named classifications.
CSLB defines C-53 for swimming pool, spa, and hot-tub construction.
CSLB documents asbestos certification for qualifying contractor classifications.
The official classification guide defines C-43 Sheet Metal Contractor.
The official guide covers pipeline work under C-34 and broader engineering work under classification A.
The official application includes B-2 Residential Remodeling for improvement projects involving existing structures.
DORA consumer guidance says not all home repair occupations are licensed statewide, including general contractors, and directs consumers to check city or county government for local rules.

limitations: Municipal and county contractor licensing remains outside OpenTrade Registry v2 board-completion scope unless separately registered.

evidence.note: DORA's home repair and remodeling guidance points consumers to city or county government for occupations not licensed statewide.

limitations: This decision does not cover statewide trade-specific licenses such as electrical, plumbing, or asbestos.

evidence.note: DORA guidance identifies general contractors as an example of occupations not licensed statewide and warns local laws may still apply.

limitations: Separate statewide trade boards may regulate work performed within commercial projects.

evidence.note: DPO states that performing electrical contracting in Colorado requires a licensed Electrical Contractor registered with the State of Colorado.
DPO lists plumber and plumbing contractor applications and licensing requirements for Colorado.
DORA consumer guidance specifically names roofers among occupations that are not licensed statewide and may be governed locally.

limitations: Local roofing registration or licensing is excluded unless separately registered as a source.

evidence.note: CDPHE states regulated asbestos abatement must be conducted by a Colorado-certified General Abatement Contractor and links to certified/registered lists.
DORA's home repair/remodeling guidance says not all occupations are licensed statewide and points consumers to local government for local protections.

limitations: This does not negate statewide licensing for trade-specific work such as electrical or plumbing.

evidence.note: Florida DBPR identifies general contracting as a Construction Industry Licensing Board registration or certification category.
Florida DBPR identifies residential contracting as a Construction Industry Licensing Board registration or certification category.
Florida DBPR's construction code catalog includes certified and registered general and building contractor classifications used for commercial building work.
Florida DBPR publishes a distinct Electrical Contractors' Licensing Board bulk file with occupation and license status fields.
Florida DBPR lists plumbing among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists air-conditioning among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists mechanical contracting among the categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists roofing among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists solar among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists swimming pool and spa categories and related statewide specialties under the construction board.
Florida DBPR publishes a distinct Asbestos Licensing Unit bulk file covering asbestos contractor occupation codes.
Florida DBPR lists sheet metal among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR lists underground utility and excavation among the contractor categories requiring registration or certification.
Florida DBPR defines a licensed construction contractor as a person who builds or improves a building or structure for compensation; local specialty credentials remain outside this source.

The official NCBEEC verify-license search lists North Carolina electrical contractor license classifications including limited, intermediate, unlimited, residential dwelling, and special restricted classifications.
The official plumbing/heating/fire-sprinkler board verify-license search lists plumbing contractor and technician classifications.
The official plumbing/heating/fire-sprinkler board verify-license search lists heating contractor and technician classifications.
TDLR Electrical Safety and Licensing FAQ states that anyone who performs electrical work in Texas must be licensed and identifies contractor license paths.
The Texas State Board of Plumbing Examiners lists statewide plumbing license types including Tradesman Plumber-Limited, Journeyman Plumber, Master Plumber, and Plumbing Inspector.
TDLR states that contractors who install, repair, or maintain air conditioning, refrigeration, or heating systems must have a TDLR license.
TDLR Air Conditioning and Refrigeration contractor licensing documentation says license class and endorsements determine the type of air conditioning and refrigeration work that may be performed.

limitations: This decision covers the TDLR ACR mechanical/HVAC path and does not resolve unrelated mechanical scopes outside TDLR ACR licensing.

evidence.note: TDLR consumer guidance for solar panels says licensed electrical contractors perform solar installation work and must hold or employ a master electrician.

limitations: This decision covers solar installation through the electrical-contractor licensing path; residential solar retail sales registration is a separate future TDLR program and is not treated as active contractor-license coverage here.
